_schema.purpose: Phase 1 spec §3b / §7 item 3. The TAIL-unique layer: how far current frontier pricing sits below true production cost = the structural repricing headroom. Derived from data/sankey_cost_structure.json:2025.validation_targets (value = true production cost; customer_revenue = what customers actually pay). multiple = value / customer_revenue.
_schema.as_of: 2025 (validation_targets vintage); re-derive on every validation_targets change (D-§8.1).
_schema.interpretation: At current pricing, customers cover `cost_recovery_pct` of true production cost; the rest is VC/parent subsidy. If subsidy unwinds AND unit cost holds, prices have ~`multiple`x structural headroom to reach cost recovery.
honesty_constraints: UPPER-BOUND SENSITIVITY, NOT A FORECAST. Assumes full customer cost-recovery, zero subsidy, unchanged unit cost.
Must be shown against the efficiency-offset counter-force (falling unit cost), never one-sided (D-§8.2).
OpenAI value is AUDITED (wq-116); Anthropic/Google value = collected_revenue + operating_loss (derived). Carry per-provider tier.

untracked.basis: Open-weight / self-hostable models are not tied to a single provider's pricing; nominal upward drift only. NOT a tracked supply-side read — illustrative floor for the price-hold scenario.

forward_signal._doc: Editorial forward-pricing narrative for the PUBLIC teaser Q2 ('Will the price hold?'). STATIC snapshot — the live, maintained per-provider signal stays gated (concept.md §5; that is the moat). Numbers transcribed from the Investment-Case work: cost_recovery_pct + repricing_multiple from validation_targets (above); underwater_pct (operating_loss / ARR), revenue_per_employee_usd_m (ARR / headcount) and valuation_to_arr from spec/five-questions-reframe.md; direction + reason prose from HA_EnterpriseAI_InvestmentCaseSimulator_Prototype. Shown WITH provenance, never presented as a live feed. The efficiency-offset counter-force is mandatory alongside this (honesty_constraints).
forward_signal.efficiency_offset: Cheaper models are improving fast — the same work already runs for a fraction on a budget model. Always show this counter-force beside the rise; the honest answer is a range, and the biggest lever is model choice.
forward_signal.corroboration: GPT-5 line actually doubled (2.0x) on 2026-04-23 — see benchmark_price_sheet.json:repricing_note.

openai.reason: They get back only about 38 cents on every dollar it costs them, and they're deep in the red. They expect to break even around 2027 — but with roughly US$25bn a year going out and a US$50bn compute bill to pay off, that money has to come back somehow. Price is the easiest lever.

anthropic.reason: They get back about 48 cents on the dollar. But they make around 4x OpenAI's sales per employee and hold most of the enterprise market — so they can grow into their costs a bit before raising prices.

google.reason: They get back only about 36 cents on the dollar — the widest gap of the three. A big parent company cushions them, so the direction is clearer than the timing.

untracked_default._doc: SPLIT 2026-07-11 (CTO update v2, 0.2): this block now applies ONLY to untracked providers whose surfaced models have open weights (provider_facts.json open_weights=true). Hosted-only untracked providers get untracked_hosted_default - the old single fallback wrongly rendered hosted models (Grok 3) as 'open-source, run in-house'.
untracked_default.direction: Low risk of a price jump
untracked_default.reason: This model's weights are openly published, so it can be run in-house and isn't tied to one provider's pricing. The risk of a sudden price jump is low. Shown as an example only — not from our own research.

untracked_hosted_default._doc: Added 2026-07-11: neutral state for untracked HOSTED providers (e.g. xAI). No risk rating implied in either direction; the price-rise column uses the same nominal 1.10 drift allowance, labelled as an allowance, never a forecast.
untracked_hosted_default.reason: We don't track this provider's finances, so there's no price forecast here — in either direction. The cost figures still work; the price-rise column carries a small nominal allowance rather than a forecast.

frontier_band._doc: Summary band across frontier providers for archetypes not pinned to one provider.
frontier_band.corroboration: GPT-5 line actually doubled (2.0x) on 2026-04-23 — see benchmark_price_sheet.json:repricing_note. Real prices moving toward this headroom, not away.
